title: Two Pigs, One Town, Same Morning — Connecticut Is Confused
script: Two loose pigs. Same morning? Woodbridge, Connecticut woke up to a problem with hooves. The first call came in at 5:45 a.m.: a pig was loose in someone's yard on Old Quarry Road. Cool, weird, whatever — until a SECOND call came in. Different street. Acorn Hill Road. Same tiny town. Same morning. That pig had apparently been living outdoors on its own for two full days. Animal control from Naugatuck and Prospect responded. A local vet came out. They caught one pig. The second one is still out there. Police are asking residents to report any sightings and NOT approach. Nobody has claimed either animal. Nobody has admitted to losing a pig. Two pigs, two streets, one town, same morning — so who loses a pig and just… doesn't notice?
